react.answer:
## Building a Tabs Component in React **Mental model:** Tabs are a single source of truth — the *active index* — driving two things: which tab looks selected and which panel renders. The accessibility contract (`role="tablist"`/`tab`/`tabpanel` + arrow-key navigation + roving `tabIndex`) is what separates a real implementation from styled buttons. ### Step 1 — Track the active index One piece of state decides everything. ```jsx const [active, setActive] = useState(0); ``` ### Step 2 — Render the tablist and panel Each tab is a ` ))}
{tabs[active].content}
``` ### Step 3 — Add arrow-key navigation Left/Right move the selection, wrapping around with modulo arithmetic. ```jsx const onKeyDown = (e) => { if (e.key === 'ArrowRight') setActive((i) => (i + 1) % tabs.length); if (e.key === 'ArrowLeft') setActive((i) => (i - 1 + tabs.length) % tabs.length); }; ``` ### Step 4 — Roving tabIndex Only the active tab is in the tab order (`tabIndex={0}`); the rest are `-1`, so Tab enters the group once and arrows move within it. ```jsx

vue.answer:
## Building a Tabs Component in Vue 3 **Mental model:** A single reactive `active` index drives both the selected-tab styling and which panel renders. Layer the ARIA roles (`tablist`/`tab`/`tabpanel`) and arrow-key navigation on top and you have a spec-compliant component rather than styled buttons. ### Step 1 — Track the active index One `ref` is the whole state. ```vue ``` ### Step 2 — Render the tablist and panel `v-for` the tabs; show only the active panel. ```vue
{{ tabs[active].content }}
``` ### Step 3 — Arrow-key navigation Handle `keydown` on the tablist and wrap with modulo. ```js function onKey(e) { if (e.key === 'ArrowRight') active.value = (active.value + 1) % tabs.length; if (e.key === 'ArrowLeft') active.value = (active.value - 1 + tabs.length) % tabs.length; } ``` ### Step 4 — Roving tabIndex Only the active tab is focusable so Tab enters the group once. ```vue

angular.answer:
## Building a Tabs Component in Angular **Mental model:** A single `active` index on the component drives both the selected-tab styling and which panel shows via `*ngIf`. Add the ARIA roles and arrow-key navigation for a spec-compliant component. Classic `NgModule` setup — no signals, no standalone, and no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them). ### Step 1 — Track the active index One number is the whole state. ```typescript @Input() tabs: Tab[] = []; @Input() initial = 0; active = 0; ngOnInit(): void { this.active = this.initial; } ``` ### Step 2 — Render tablist and panel `*ngFor` the tabs with `[attr.aria-selected]`; show the active panel. ```html
{{ tabs[active].content }}
``` ### Step 3 — Arrow-key navigation Handle `(keydown)` on the tablist and wrap with modulo. ```typescript onKey(e: KeyboardEvent): void { if (e.key === 'ArrowRight') this.active = (this.active + 1) % this.tabs.length; if (e.key === 'ArrowLeft') this.active = (this.active - 1 + this.tabs.length) % this.tabs.length; } ``` ### Step 4 — Roving tabindex Only the active tab is focusable. ```html
